title: Contradiction Quiz: Where Do You Stand?
description: A self-test – not a pigeonhole, but a mirror. Answer the questions and discover how you think.

questions.text: You read a controversial article. Your first impulse is:

options.text: Immediately agree or disagree – I know where I stand.
Check whether the arguments hold – even if I don't like the result.
See what the majority thinks before I take a position.
Share the article because it provokes – regardless of whether I agree.

questions.text: In a group discussion, someone holds a position you consider wrong. You:

options.text: Say clearly why you see it differently – even if it gets uncomfortable.
Stay silent to avoid provoking conflict.
Try to convince them – you know what's right.
Ask: What exactly do you mean? How did you arrive at that?

questions.text: AI can now generate text, images, and code. For you this means:

options.text: Finally less work – AI should take over as much as possible.
AI is dangerous and should be regulated more strictly.
AI is a tool. What matters is who uses it for what.
AI shows that human judgment becomes more valuable, not less.

questions.text: You have to make an important decision but don't have all the information. You:

options.text: Wait until you have all the facts – safety first.
Decide anyway – with the best judgment you have right now.
Ask someone you trust and follow their advice.
Decide quickly and clearly – hesitation is weakness.

questions.text: What does education mean to you?

options.text: Acquiring knowledge, passing exams, enabling a career.
Conveying the right values and changing society.
Learning to distinguish, judge, and act better.
A protected space for reflection and experimentation.

questions.text: Someone says: "You have to choose – left or right." You think:

options.text: True. Neutrality is cowardly.
Not true. The most important questions cannot be forced into camps.
I'd rather stay out of political discussions.
It depends – sometimes you need to take a stand, sometimes to differentiate.

questions.text: Your favourite word from this list:

options.text: Security
Justice
Judgment
Contradiction

mitte.title: Radical Middle
mitte.description: You think before you react. You endure contradiction and seek judgment rather than a camp. The Atelier is designed exactly for people like you.

lager.title: Camp Thinker
lager.description: You have clear convictions and take positions quickly. That can be a strength – but also a closing off. The Atelier invites you to stress-test your positions.

anpassung.title: Conformity Artist
anpassung.description: You seek safety and consensus. That is understandable – but sometimes too little. The Atelier wants to help you endure uncertainty and still act.
